articles.title: Nabard pulls out bond issuance on weak demand
articles.description: In a surprising turn of events, Nabard has pulled the plug on its scheduled bond sale this Friday, citing a lackluster response from investors. The rural development bank aimed to secure ₹7,000 crore but only attracted bids close to ₹3,030 crore. Analysts suggest that the borrowing costs would have ranged between 7.79% and 8.

Investors are advised to hold tight until market indicators become more distinct. The persistent geopolitical climate is keeping oil prices robust, creating challenges for company profitability.

articles.title: License to deliver: Some midwives break the law to assist with home births

The room next door, where Collins' appointment began, was filled with children's toys and scented candles and warmed by a wood-burning stove.

Xbox is now XBOX
articles.description:
Xbox just allcapsmaxxed: meet XBOX. This isn't a joke, Microsoft appears to be actually rebranding Xbox to XBOX. Asha Sharma, Xbox CEO, ran a poll on X earlier this week, asking fans whether Microsoft should use Xbox or XBOX? The results were in favor of XBOX, and the company has now renamed its X account.
Curiously, the Threads and Bluesky accounts for Xbox haven't been renamed yet, but if Microsoft is going ahead with a rebranding then I'd expect those will change soon. 
The use of all caps for Xbox is a return to original form. Microsoft's first Xbox logo for its console was all caps, and the company has favored using similar capped ver …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: YouTube is expanding its AI deepfake detection tool to all adult users
articles.description:
YouTube is expanding its AI likeness detection program to all users over the age of 18 - meaning just about anyone can have the platform hunt for potential deepfakes of themselves.
The likeness detection feature uses a selfie-style scan of a person's face to monitor YouTube for lookalikes. If there is a match, YouTube alerts the user; the person then has the option to request that YouTube remove the content. YouTube has said in the past that it has found the number of removal requests to be "very small."
YouTube began testing the feature with content creators, and then expanded it to government officials, politicians, journalists, and fina …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: Windows 11 tests an adjustable taskbar and resizable Start menu
articles.description:
Microsoft's latest Windows 11 test will allow you to reposition the taskbar and change the size of the Start menu. The update, which is rolling out to Windows 11 Insiders in the Experimental channel, lets you place the taskbar on the bottom, top, left, or right side of the screen.
Microsoft first teased its movable taskbar in March as part of efforts to rebuild trust among users. You can adjust the alignment of the icons inside the taskbar, as well as open the Start menu drawer from wherever you placed it. Windows 11 Insiders can access a shorter taskbar, too, which could come in handy for devices with smaller displays. There's also an opti …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: ArXiv will ban researchers who upload papers full of AI slop
articles.description:
ArXiv, a popular platform for preprint academic research, is taking a new step to attempt to reduce the volume of papers that include AI slop.
If a paper has "incontrovertible evidence that the authors did not check the results of LLM generation," such as hallucinated references or "meta-comments" left by an LLM, authors will be banned from ArXiv for a year, according to Thomas Dietterich, ArXiv's section chair of its computer science section. Future ArXiv submissions will also have to be accepted at "a reputable peer-reviewed venue."
Here's what he said on X:
Attention @arxiv authors: Our Code of Conduct states that by signing your name …

Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.description: The stock split will be processed the week of May 18 and is expected to be completed by May 22.

articles.title: Visakhapatnam zoo summer camp to begin from May 19
articles.description: IGZP to host five-day summer camp with wildlife learning, birdwatching and interactive activities for children

articles.title: Tech Wrap: iPhone 18 Pro Colours, iOS 26.5 Rollout, Motorola Razr Fold
articles.description: From iPhone 18 Pro rumours to Instagram Instants launch, here's a roundup of the week's biggest developments in tech.

articles.title: Nabard pulls out bond issuance on weak demand
articles.description: In a surprising turn of events, Nabard has pulled the plug on its scheduled bond sale this Friday, citing a lackluster response from investors. The rural development bank aimed to secure ₹7,000 crore but only attracted bids close to ₹3,030 crore. Analysts suggest that the borrowing costs would have ranged between 7.79% and 8.

articles.title: Pakistan lose eight WTC points for slow over-rate in first Test against Bangladesh
articles.description: Pakistan became the second team to be docked points for bowling their overs slowly in the ongoing WTC cycle after England lost two points during the Lord's Test last year

articles.title: Fuel hike not enough to offset all losses, oil stocks fall further
articles.description: In an unexpected turn, shares of oil companies fell even with a recent fuel price boost. This adjustment couldn't compensate for the rising crude oil prices, leaving experts calling for further alterations in pricing. Investors are advised to hold tight until market indicators become more distinct. The persistent geopolitical climate is keeping oil prices robust, creating challenges for company profitability.

articles.title: Long Island Rail Workers Strike in First Walkout Since 1994
articles.description: The Long Island Rail Road, the nation’s busiest commuter line, will suspend service for the first time in more than 30 years after labor groups and transit officials failed to reach a deal on wage increases by a Friday night deadline.

articles.title: Long Island Rail Road workers go on strike after failing to reach new contract
articles.description: The strike stemmed from a four-year contract​. The MTA and unions agreed to the terms of the first three years, but disputes arose about the final year.

topStories.description: At a meeting with the Trinamool Congress' contesting candidates, she said the organisation would rise again despite suffering a crushing defeat in the recently concluded assembly elections.

topStories.title: New York doctor who survived Ebola says he fears for healthcare workers
topStories.description: A New York doctor who contracted and survived Ebola more than a decade ago says he is worried for healthcare workers who are at the center of treating the latest outbreak.

topStories.title: Route 66 centennial spurs tourism in historic small towns
topStories.description: A few small towns along U.S. Route 66 crumbled when it was decommissioned in the 1980s, but the road's 100th anniversary this year has rebirthed tourism along the historic route.

topStories.description: The US president says he wants Beijing and Taipei to "cool down" tensions over the self-governing island.

topStories.description: Caitlin Clark scored 32 points and hit a clutch three-pointer to force overtime, but the Indiana Fever fell to the Washington Mystics 104-102 on Friday.

dailyNews.description: Two days of talks in Washington between Lebanon and Israel produced an extension of the current ceasefire by 45 days.

dailyNews.title: Colorado governor commutes Trump ally Tina Peters' prison sentence
dailyNews.description: Colorado's governor on Friday announced he is commuting the sentence of former Mesa County Clerk Tina Peters.

dailyNews.title: Senator John Kennedy introduces America to ‘Margaret,’ his elliptical trainer named after Thatcher
dailyNews.description: Sen. John Kennedy posts a tongue-in-cheek video introducing his elliptical trainer named Margaret after Margaret Thatcher, going viral on social media.

dailyNews.title: Washington Nationals unveil the latest stadium snack that will excite some, horrify others
dailyNews.description: The Washington Nationals' Hot Dog Day featured hot dog fries, shoestring-style fried wieners with chipotle ranch, sparking debate among baseball fans.

dailyNews.title: Waymo driverless cars overrun Atlanta neighborhood, circling cul-de-sacs and alarming families with kids
dailyNews.description: Atlanta's Buckhead neighborhood was overrun by empty Waymo driverless cars circling through cul-de-sacs due to a routing behavior issue.

dailyNews.title: ‘Perfect statement’: Trump defends saying he isn’t thinking about Americans’ finances amid Iran talks
dailyNews.description: The president also said he’s not considering the political implications of the Iran war on the midterms, when Republicans will be defending slim majorities in each chamber.

dailyNews.title: Remains of missing 6-year-old boy found years after his alleged murder
dailyNews.description: Noel Rodriguez-Alvarez was last seen alive in October 2022, according to police in Everman, Texas, where the family lived at the time.

dailyNews.title: Analysis shows how using obesity drugs for weight loss is associated with a clinically relevant drop in blood pressure
dailyNews.description: A meta-analysis of 32 studies and 43,618 adults presented at the European Congress on Obesity in Istanbul, Turkey (May 12–15) shows that use of new classes of obesity drugs for weight loss is associated with a clinically relevant drop in blood pressure (BP): a 0.34 mmHg reduction in systolic BP per 1% weight loss.

dailyNews.title: FDA shuffles top drug, biologics leaders in latest shakeup
dailyNews.description: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration is shuffling its top drug and biologics regulators, days after the departure of former commissioner Marty Makary.

dailyNews.title: New Ebola outbreak leaves 65 dead as officials warn of cross-border spread
dailyNews.description: A new Ebola outbreak in Congo has killed 65 people with 246 suspected cases as officials investigate which strain is driving the deadly spread.

dailyNews.title: Prominent N.Y. synagogue targeted in alleged terror plot, prosecutors say
dailyNews.description: An Iraqi national allegedly plotted to carry out terror attacks in the U.S., including at a prominent synagogue in New York, prosecutors said Friday.

dailyNews.title: New obesity guidance urges dietitian-led care as GLP-1 drugs reshape treatment
dailyNews.description: Obesity and dietitian societies have joined forces to issue a new consensus statement on recommendations surrounding the use of obesity drugs for weight loss treatment. The consensus statement was presented at the European Congress on Obesity (ECO 2026) in Istanbul, Turkey, and co-authored by lead author Dr. Laurence Dobbie, Department of Population Health Sciences, School of Life Course & Population Sciences, King's College London, UK as part of an international team of 26 authors.

dailyNews.title: Mahmoud Khalil calls for deportation to be halted in light of new evidence
dailyNews.description: Lawyers say new evidence suggests Trump admin 'engineered' outcome of case against pro-Palestine Columbia student.

dailyNews.title: Colorado's Democratic governor will let Trump ally Tina Peters out of prison early
dailyNews.description: Gov. Jared Polis' controversial commutation follows a pressure campaign by the Trump administration to free Tina Peters, an ex-county official who was convicted of tampering with election equipment.

dailyNews.title: Trump touted Palantir on Truth Social after buying the company's stock, records show
dailyNews.description: During the first three months of the year, Trump purchased between $630,000 and $247,008 worth of Palantir stock and sold at least $1.1 million.

dailyNews.title: Trump says Abu-Bilal al-Minuki, second in command of ISIS globally, killed in US-Nigerian operation
dailyNews.description: President Trump announced U.S. and Nigerian forces eliminated Abu-Bilal al-Minuki, whom he identified as the second in command of ISIS globally.

dailyNews.title: White House gets an unexpected buzz as thousands of bees swarm the executive mansion's North Lawn
dailyNews.description: Thousands of bees swarmed the White House North Lawn Friday, weeks after Melania Trump announced the addition of two new bee colonies to the property.

dailyNews.title: Colorado governor commutes Tina Peters' sentence as Trump posts ‘FREE TINA!’
dailyNews.description: Colorado Gov. Jared Polis commutes former election clerk Tina Peters' sentence, drawing praise from Trump and backlash from Secretary of State Griswold.

dailyNews.title: Trump’s ‘Learning Curve’ on China Ends With Conciliation at Summit
dailyNews.description: The president has shifted the foundations of American policy toward China, throwing aside the adversarial approach of recent years.

dailyNews.title: Why Were These C.E.O.s in Beijing With Trump?
dailyNews.description: Some of America’s most powerful C.E.O.s accompanied President Trump to Beijing during his summit with President Xi Jinping of China. Our reporter Ana Swanson explains what they were hoping to gain from the trip.

dailyNews.title: Xi Pitches His Vision for Avoiding Conflict With the U.S.
dailyNews.description: Having fought the Trump administration to a draw, China’s Xi Jinping is proposing “constructive strategic stability,” aimed at drawing lines he thinks the U.S. should not cross.

dailyNews.title: For Xi’s Critics, Trump’s China Visit Is Fuel for Jokes They Can’t Tell
dailyNews.description: On Threads and other sites, liberal-minded Chinese accounts were mocking the proceedings and offering a rare window into opinions on Xi Jinping and his leadership style.

dailyNews.title: Back From China, Trump Faces Decision on Whether to Resume Strikes on Iran
dailyNews.description: Top aides have drafted battle plans as peace negotiations have stalled.
